rationale: control_dependence: failure -- C01-P01 (an incorrect heater-shaker module name is corrected after Coscientist consults the Docs searcher module and modifies the protocol to a corrected version, which ran successfully), C01-P35 (the language model behind the Planner 'enables code to be fixed in case of software errors'). observation_result -- C01-P90/C01-P91 (the yield-maximization 'game' selects reaction conditions 'while listing the player[']s observations', i.e. conditioned on prior-round results), C01-P08 ('the strategy of Coscientist changes among runs'), C01-P09 ('normalized advantage values increase over time') across the optimization run. No fixed_or_predefined and no not_reported: every reported selection step is conditioned on an observed error or yield, and the source states these dependences, so neither outcome-independence (fixed_or_predefined) nor source silence (not_reported) applies. No score axis at the policy step: the 1-5 compound-synthesis scale (C01-P40) benchmarks baseline LLMs against each other and is not shown feeding Coscientist's own action selection; the yield the game maximizes is an executed reaction outcome, recorded as observation_result. selection_signal names the concrete drivers: the software/execution error that triggers a fix (C01-P01/P35), the reaction yield the game maximizes (C01-P90), the prior-round observations each move is conditioned on (C01-P90/P91), and the normalized advantage value tracked across the run (C01-P09). selection_objective: local_repair -- the error corrections (C01-P01/P35) restore a working procedure locally. performance_improvement -- C01-P90 states the game's explicit objective is 'maximizing the reaction yield'; no source proposition frames selection as targeting uncertainty reduction or hypothesis discrimination, and no novelty/diversity mechanism is reported, so C01 carries neither uncertainty_reduction, hypothesis_model_discrimination, nor diversity_directed_selection. generation_scope: fixed_space -- Coscientist selects within a fixed, pre-scoped set of reaction conditions (C01-P89 'any reaction proposed by Coscientist would be within these datasets and accessible as a lookup table'; C01-P91 'selecting specific reaction conditions'); no search-space-expansion or candidate-diversification mechanism is reported (contrast C03/C04/C06). candidate_selection_rule: sequential_choice -- the yield-optimization game selects conditions as sequential moves, each conditioned on the accumulated observations (C01-P90/P91); the selection is yield-directed in objective but proceeds as a sequential game rather than a one-shot top-k ranking or an exhaustive sweep. candidate_execution_rule: one_at_a_time -- each selected reaction condition is executed as a single game move before the next is chosen (C01-P91); until_success -- the repair loop re-runs the corrected code until it executes successfully (C01-P01 'modifies the protocol to a corrected version, which ran successfully'; C01-P35 'enables code to be fixed'). No objective label appears in either rule. formal_epistemic_utility_evidence: not_reported -- no proposition states a prior/posterior, likelihood, or expected-information/epistemic-utility term used directly in a selection rule (corpus-level footnote, stated once across all six cases).
